section.p: In this step, we'll estimate your and your spouse's joint earned income, adjusted gross income, and any adjustments to it.
Please select and enter your and your spouse's income and adjustments. Only select one item per source. All fields marked with an asterisk are required.

div.p: Please note: Make sure to also include your spouse's income and adjustments.

fg-set.question: Wages, salary, or tips where federal income taxes are withheld
fg-set.modal-link: Wages Salaries and Tips / Military & Combat Pay

fg-detail.summary: Work with no federal taxes withheld

fg-set.question: Work where federal income taxes were not withheld
fg-set.modal-link: What are jobs where federal taxes aren't withheld?

fg-set.question: Self-employment Gross income
Total self-employment work-related expenses you and your spouse deducted in
fg-set.hint: It is okay to leave this blank.

fg-set.question: Disability retirement benefits before minimum retirement age
Foreign earned income not reported on Form 2555

section.p: Even if you or your spouse earned a small amount of investment income, adding this amount helps get the most accurate calculation.

p.modal-link: What is interest income and how can I calculate it?

fg-set.question: Interest income you and your spouse earned in

fg-detail.p: You should enter the total amounts you earned in .

p.modal-link: What are dividends and how can I calculate them?

fg-set.question: Ordinary dividend income you and your spouse earned in
Qualified dividend income you and your spouse earned in

fg-detail.summary: Short-term or long-term gains and losses
fg-detail.p: You should enter the total amounts you earned and lost in .

p.modal-link: What are short- and long-term gains and losses, and how can I calculate them?

fg-set.question: Short-term gains you and your spouse earned in
Short-term losses you and your spouse had in
Long-term gains you and your spouse earned in
Long-term losses you and your spouse had in

p.modal-link: What are rental expenses and how can I calculate net rental income?

fg-set.question: Net amount of rental income you and your spouse earned in
fg-set.hint: Net is the total minus any expenses.

p.modal-link: How can I calculate royalty income?

fg-set.question: Royalty income you and your spouse earned in

fg-detail.summary: Passive or non-passive income from S-corporations or partnerships
fg-detail.p: You should enter the total amounts you earned in .

p.modal-link: How can I calculate income from S-corporations or partnerships?

fg-set.question: Passive income from S-corporations or partnerships you and your spouse earned in
Non-passive income from S-corporations or partnerships you and your spouse earned in
Taxable portion of any pension
fg-set.modal-link: What is the taxable portion of a pension?

fg-set.question: Total distributions you and your spouse received from pre-tax retirement accounts in
fg-set.hint: Don't include distributions from Roth (post-tax) accounts.

fg-set.question: Unemployment insurance

fg-detail.summary: Social Security and railroad retirement benefits

fg-set.question: Taxable portion of Social Security and Railroad Retirement Benefits
fg-set.modal-link: What counts as Social Security and Railroad Retirement Benefits?

fg-set.question: A scholarship or grant that must be counted as income
fg-set.modal-link: What counts as a scholarship or grant?

fg-set.question: Pay received for work while an inmate in a penal institution
Total amount of any other income you and your spouse received in
fg-set.hint: You should enter one total amount for all other income.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: Wages, Salaries, and Tips / Military & Disability

modal-content.p: Your employer reported your wages, salaries, tips, and other compensation in box 1 of your Form W-2.
Include the full amount of tip and overtime income in the calculation of earned income when determining eligibility for the earned income tax credit, even if all or part is deductible.
Include all amounts reported in box 1 of Form W-2 for both you and your spouse if you're filing a joint tax return with your spouse.
Don't include your Form W-2 pre-tax contributions in the pre-tax contributions section.
You can also choose to include your nontaxable combat pay in earned income for the EITC. Your employer reported your nontaxable combat pay in box 12 with code Q on your Form W-2.

This choice may increase or decrease your EITC. Calculate your EITC both ways to see which option provides the greater benefit. For more information, see Nontaxable combat pay in Publication 596, Earned Income Credit.
If you took parental leave or were temporarily disabled during the year and your employer paid you, they reported your income on your W-2. Enter that income here.
Include temporary or short-term disability in your wages.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: Income Where Federal Taxes Weren't Withheld

modal-content.p: Report income earned from gig work, even if it's a side job, part time or temporary.
Enter income here if you earn money from a job even if income taxes were not withheld. You may or may not have received a Form 1099 for all income.
The income may have been earned through an app or website to:

ul.li: Drive a car for booked rides or deliveries
Provide other temporary, on-demand or freelance work

modal-content.p: Your gross income includes all the income you received. We will subtract your expenses later.
For more information, visit Manage Taxes for Your Gig Work

ul.li: Are self-employed (You may or may not receive a Form 1099 for all income)
Are a minister or member of a religious order

modal-content.p: Your gross income includes all the income you received. We will subtract your expenses later.
For more information, visit Self-employed individuals tax center

If you retired on disability, we consider the taxable benefits you received under your employer's disability retirement plan earned income for the purposes of the EITC until you reach minimum retirement age.
The minimum retirement age is the earliest age at which you could have received a pension or annuity if you weren't disabled.
Pay you received for work you performed while an inmate in a penal institution isn't earned income when figuring the earned income credit.
This includes paid work performed while in a work release program or while in a halfway house.
For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Earnings while an inmate.
Foreign earned income is income earned while living and working outside the United States, District of Columbia, or U.S. possessions or territories.
If you’re a U.S. government employee working outside the United States, that income is not considered foreign earned.
If you're not a government employee and you work at an embassy, we consider that income as foreign income for the EITC.
For more information, see Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income.
Note: If you elect to file Form 2555, you are not eligible to claim the EITC.
If you receive retirement benefits in the form of pension or annuity payments from a qualified employer retirement plan, all or some portion of the amounts you receive may be taxable.

ul.li: You didn't contribute anything or aren't considered to have contributed anything for your pension or annuity
Your employer didn't withhold contributions from your salary, or
You received all your contributions tax-free in prior years
You contributed after-tax dollars (use the General Rule and Simplified Method to calculate)

modal-content.p: Additional rules apply if you received payments before age 59 and a half, are a survivor or beneficiary, or have other situations. For more information visit Tax Topic 410.
For the EITC, include your full unemployment compensation benefits. You find this income on Form 1099-G.
If your employer's fund provided supplemental benefits, include any amounts reported on Form W-2, box 1.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: Social Security and Railroad Retirement Benefits

modal-content.p: For the EITC, include only the taxable amount of your Social Security and railroad retirement benefits.
IRS Notice 703 can help you determine if any of your Social Security benefits are taxable. For more information visit Tax Topic 423.
To compute the taxable amount of your benefits, see "Which worksheet to use" in Publication 915, Social Security and Equivalent Railroad Retirement Benefits.
Enter any amount of taxable scholarship not reported on your Form W-2.
This amount includes scholarship or grant money not found on your W-2 if the money was:

ul.li: Used for incidental expenses, such as room and board, travel, and optional equipment
Received as payment for teaching, research, or other services required as a condition for receiving the scholarship or fellowship grant

modal-content.p: Don't include amounts you received for services required by the National Health Service Corps Scholarship Program, the Armed Forces Health Professions Scholarship and Financial Assistance Program, or a comprehensive student work-learning-service program operated by a work college.
For more information visit Tax Topic 421.

fg-alert.heading: You don’t qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
fg-alert.p: To claim the EITC, you must have had earned income (for example, wages or net earnings from self-employment). Based on the income you entered, your earned income for was zero or less, so you don’t qualify for the EITC.
For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit.

fg-alert.heading: You don’t qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)

div.p: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) if your investment income was more than .
For more information, see Publication 550, Investment Income and Expenses. To figure your total investment income, you should use Worksheet 1 in Publication 596, Earned Income Credit.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What did it mean to deduct expenses?

modal-content.p: When you were self employed, a gig worker, or owned and operated a business, you may have been able to deduct certain business expenses from your income so you paid less tax.
Business expenses can include:
These deductions are different from the personal deductions you made if you chose to itemize your deductions. You can deduct your business expenses here and still choose the standard deduction later.

p.strong: How to check last year's tax return?

modal-content.p: If you were self-employed in you can find your deductible expenses on line 28 of Schedule C.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What counts as a distribution from pre-tax retirement accounts?

modal-content.p: Any withdrawal of pre-tax funds from a 401(k), 403(b), 457(b), or traditional Individual Retirement Arrangement (IRA) counts as a distribution. This included distributions at retirement, early withdrawals, required minimum distributions (RMDs), and pretax-to-Roth conversions.
At the end of the year, your financial institution sent a Form 1099-R to report this income.

ul.li: Other forms of retirement income, like pensions, annuities, Social Security, or other investment income. You should enter them in other sections.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What was interest income and how can I calculate it?

modal-content.p: Common types of interest income include interest earned from bank accounts or from lending money.

p.strong: How to check last year's tax return

modal-content.p: If you earned interest income in , check for an amount in:

ul.li: Line 2b on Form 1040 of your federal tax return

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What are dividends and how can I calculate them?

modal-content.p: Ordinary or qualified dividends are payments made by corporations to shareholders based on a company's profits. The main difference between the two was that qualified dividends are taxed at a lower rate.

p.strong: How to check last year's tax return

modal-content.p: If you earned these dividends the previous year and thought you would earn a similar amount in , check:

ul.li: Line 3b on Form 1040 of your federal tax return for ordinary dividends
Line 3a on Form 1040 of your federal tax return for qualified dividends

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What are short- and long-term gains and losses, and how can I calculate them?

modal-content.p: The length of time you held an asset determined what type of gain or loss it was:

ul.li: Short-term gain or loss, if you held the asset for less than a year.
Long-term gain or loss, if you held the asset for more than one year.

p.strong: How to check for gains and losses

modal-content.p: If you had an investment or brokerage account, you can log in or reviewed your latest statement to check for any short- or long-term gains and losses for .

p.strong: How to calculate gains and losses

modal-content.p: If you sold any assets before the end of the year, you should include the calculated gains or losses in your total.

modal-dialog.modal-heading: What are rental expenses and how can I calculate net rental income?

modal-content.p: Rental expenses (opens in a new tab) included maintenance costs, insurance, taxes, and interest.

p.strong: How to check last year's tax return

modal-content.p: If you earned rental income during , check your forms:

ul.li: Schedule E (Form 1040), Part I, line 21, Columns A - C

modal-dialog.modal-heading: How can I calculate royalty income?

modal-content.p: You can check your licensing agreements or publisher statements for expected payment schedules or percentages.

p.strong: How to check last year's tax return

modal-content.p: If you earned royalties in , check your forms:

modal-dialog.modal-heading: How can I calculate income from S-corporations or partnerships?

p.strong: How to check last year's return

modal-content.p: If you earned income from S-corporations or partnerships the previous year and thought you would earn a similar amount in , check these forms:

ul.li: Schedule E (Form 1040), Part II, Line 29a, Column h, for passive income
Schedule E (Form 1040), Part II, Line 29a, Column k, for non-passive income

modal-content.p: Add any other taxable income that you and your spouse received in , including:

ul.li: Court awards and damages (unless they were for physical injury or sickness)

p.a: Learn more about other taxable income. (opens in a new tab)
